page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU includes 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix mathematics. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in single-core operations, with Geekbench single-core and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. The hardware demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptographic tasks, image processing, and text processing. Memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also in the top 25%, while read bandwidth remains in the middle 50%. In LLM inference tests, the server achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for text generation using small 135M models, though performance drops to the bottom 25% when executing text generation on large 70B models.

The m7i-flex.8xlarge offers a resource-dense configuration that balances dedicated compute power with high-speed DDR5 memory. This makes it a cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads, such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, which do not require local NVMe storage. However, the lack of local storage and the baseline network speed of 6 Gbps represent key tradeoffs for data-intensive or high-throughput clustering applications. It is best suited for application servers, medium-scale database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While the server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it delivers strong single-core performance, high-speed memory write bandwidth, and efficient text processing, as shown in benchmark tests. It achieves top-tier speeds for small-model LLM text generation, though performance drops significantly on larger models. With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, this instance is optimized for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose server with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C CPU.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for versatile workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor
128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix math
Strong single-core CPU, cryptography, and text processing benchmark results
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local NVMe storage
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
